agent_notes: Bromley produced a quality-led shortlist below the 30-35 target: strong craft beer, park-walk, and market options, but very few qualifying Books & Coffee venues and only one museum/gallery day with a current exhibition plus recent press.
Creative Walks are limited to Beckenham Place Park and Crystal Palace Park because both are explicitly on the PAINTS v6 safety list; no off-list Bromley parks were used.
Google Maps ratings/review counts and exact group walk-in acceptance could not be programmatically verified from public Google pages; affected fields are marked UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.opening_hours: Official site: Mon-Wed 15:00-22:00, Thu 14:00-22:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-22:00, Sun 12:00-21:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site says walk-ins and bookings are both welcome; group of 8-12 tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Seating for 50 is stated by venue; 8-12 likely feasible off-peak, but host should call ahead.
venues.capacity_notes: Venue states seating for 50, 20 beers on tap, 6 cask beers and 150 fridge beers.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 13:00-16:00 or Sunday 13:00-16:00 before peak evening trade.
venues.why_fits_paints: A proper neighbourhood beer cafe with local, national and international independent breweries rather than a generic pub. The Clock House location gives it useful outer-south texture. It has enough range for a show-and-tell format where each attendee can bring a bottle, label, or taste note into conversation.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA notes it opened in 2020 during the Covid crisis and was able to open more fully in summer 2021; the site frames it as Beckenham's original craft beer bar and microbrewery.
venues.serve_or_on_view: 20 beers on tap, 6 cask beers, around 150 fridge beers, plus wine, cider and spirits.
venues.safety_notes: Directly by Clock House station and bus routes; evening exit is straightforward via Beckenham Road. Host should keep arrival/departure on lit main-road routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8-10 min walk via Beckenham Road / Kelsey Park Road - host to verify on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Popular small beer venue; call ahead for 8-12 even though walk-ins are welcomed.

venues.opening_hours: Official site: Mon-Thu 16:00-22:00, Fri 16:00-22:00, Sat 12:00-22:00, Sun 12:00-21:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations are available and must be made at least 6 hours in advance; walk-in acceptance for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Small taproom format; feasible only with host heads-up.
venues.capacity_notes: CAMRA describes a microbrewery taproom with 10 keg taps, rotating guests, enclosed pavement seating, awning and heaters.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a compact local brewery/taproom rather than a polished high-street chain. The production story gives the host something concrete to discuss. It pairs neatly with nearby Beckenham indie drinking backups.
venues.anecdote_story: BR3WERY moved production to a nearby former small industrial unit in 2024 while keeping the public bar presence in Beckenham.
venues.serve_or_on_view: House beers, guest keg lines, and pavement seating.
venues.safety_notes: Main Beckenham streets and nearby rail/tram options; use lit routes toward Beckenham Junction or Clock House after dark.
venues.backup_venue: Grand Cru Co, 9 Kelsey Park Road
venues.risks_watchouts: Walk-in policy for larger groups is not explicit; call before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Opening hours not found on official site; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Instagram showed recent activity in June 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm; official site presents it as a wine shop and lounge.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable for a wine-led group if arranged in advance; walk-in group tolerance UNVERIFIED.
venues.capacity_notes: Official site says the shop/lounge offers over 500 wines.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday early evening or Saturday late afternoon, subject to confirmation.
venues.why_fits_paints: A wine shop and lounge with real browsing depth gives attendees a tangible object of conversation: bottle labels, regions, and personal taste. It is quieter and more discovery-led than a standard bar. The immediate backup density on Kelsey Park Road helps operations.
venues.anecdote_story: The strongest story is curatorial rather than historic: an independent Beckenham wine merchant built around a deep bottle range and tasting/lounge use.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Over 500 wines, artisan sharing boards and light bites per official/Instagram sources.
venues.safety_notes: Central Beckenham, short walk to station corridors; host should avoid late dispersal through the park.
venues.risks_watchouts: Hours and walk-in/group policy require direct confirmation; keep as Tier A only if confirmed.

venues.opening_hours: Official taproom page: Mon-Thu 17:00-22:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-21:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations are possible through booking sources; walk-ins for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely feasible early weekend in taproom; call ahead for group seating.
venues.capacity_notes: CAMRA describes a hidden brewery taproom in a 19th-century warehouse with outdoor area.
venues.why_fits_paints: Southey has the right off-grid feel: a brewery down a side street, with real production heritage and a local-loop identity. It is characterful without being performative. Good for an object-led drinks conversation around brewing, labels, and local industrial texture.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA traces the site from Late Knights brewery in 2013 to Southey restarting brewing in December 2016, using a 6 barrel kit.
venues.serve_or_on_view: House beers, taproom pours, takeaway cans, Penge Gin, and events.
venues.safety_notes: Close to Penge High Street; host should keep group arrival/departure on main lit streets from Penge East or Penge West.
venues.risks_watchouts: Side-street location is a vibe advantage but needs clear host wayfinding after dark.

venues.opening_hours: Official page: Mon 17:00-21:00, Tue-Thu 16:00-22:00, Fri 14:00-23:00, Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 14:00-20:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for 8-12; public taproom/shop opening hours are listed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely feasible at non-peak times, but seating/capacity for 8-12 not verified.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent craft beer pub/shop on Penge High Street; CAMRA notes independent status.
venues.why_fits_paints: A focused craft-beer shop/taproom works for show-and-tell because the shelves and taps provide conversational props. It is less story-rich than Southey or Three Hounds, so it sits as Tier B. Penge gives the list needed geographic counterweight within Bromley.
venues.anecdote_story: No deep venue backstory found beyond independent craft beer positioning and Penge high-street presence.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft beer taps and packaged beer.
venues.safety_notes: High Street location near stations; host should avoid dispersing through quieter residential side streets late.
venues.risks_watchouts: Capacity and group walk-ins need confirmation; Tier B because the story signal is thinner.

venues.opening_hours: Official site: Mon 15:00-22:00, Tue-Thu 12:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-00:00, Sun 12:00-22:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub walk-ins likely, but 8-12 group seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely feasible off-peak; event/function room exists but would require booking.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional pub with bottle shop, beer festival activity and function room information.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Bromley town-centre pub with a serious beer/bottle-shop angle is a better PAINTS fit than generic high-street bars. It has enough beer culture for attendees to bring tasting notes and personal associations. Operationally it is central and backed by another craft beer venue nearby.
venues.anecdote_story: Londonist highlights the Star & Garter for ales, cider and beer-garden pub features; 2026 beer festival listings indicate an active beer culture.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ale, cider, bottle shop stock, beer festival programming.
venues.safety_notes: Central Bromley High Street with Bromley North/South access; standard town-centre evening awareness applies.
venues.backup_venue: The Craft Beer Cabin, 18 London Road
venues.risks_watchouts: Check football/event clashes and function-room private hire before listing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site directs users to Google for up-to-date opening hours; CAMRA says opens Wed-Sun from 10:30 for coffee and pastries as from 19 Feb 2025. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for group walk-in.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Small craft beer cabin/shop format; likely tight for 8-12, hence Tier B.
venues.capacity_notes: CAMRA lists it as a Bromley pub/craft beer venue with Instagram @craftbeercabin.
venues.why_fits_paints: The bottle-shop/cabin model gives tactile browsing and show-and-tell prompts. It is more operationally fragile than a larger pub. Best used as a backup or small-group version of a Bromley beer event.
venues.anecdote_story: No strong origin story found; the useful PAINTS angle is its specialist beer-cabin format.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft beer plus coffee/pastry opening noted by CAMRA.
venues.safety_notes: London Road is a main route close to Bromley North; use lit town-centre paths.
venues.backup_venue: The Star and Garter, 227 High Street
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening hours, seating and group walk-in policy are insufficiently published.

venues.opening_hours: Official contact page: Mon closed, Tue-Wed 16:30-22:00, Thu 16:30-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 14:00-20:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site says now taking reservations and invites customers to visit during normal business hours; walk-ins for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Social dining boards suit 8-12, but likely needs advance call; reservation leaning keeps it Tier B.
venues.capacity_notes: Family-owned cheese, charcuterie and wine bar; TripAdvisor shows 4.9 from 15 reviews, below PAINTS Google review-count gate without further verification.
venues.why_fits_paints: Build-your-own boards create natural show-and-tell structure around taste, texture and provenance. The Chislehurst high-street setting diversifies the Bromley shortlist. It is capped at Tier B because group walk-in and review-count gates need stronger proof.
venues.anecdote_story: Visit Chislehurst describes it as a family-owned social dining venue built around custom charcuterie boards and curated British/European cheeses.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cheese boards, charcuterie, tapas, wines and cocktails.
venues.safety_notes: Chislehurst High Street is a conventional evening setting; host should coordinate station/taxi departures because rail is not immediately outside.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying PAINTS-coded drinks backup found within 10 min - closest likely alternatives are traditional pubs on Chislehurst High Street, but they lack the required independent wine/craft signal.
venues.risks_watchouts: No verified qualifying backup; review-count and walk-in gates require host confirmation.

venues.venue_name: Beckenham Place Park Food & Farmers Market
venues.opening_hours: Market page and Instagram: Sundays 10:00-15:00. Park open 24 hours year-round. Severe weather/private event changes UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; N/A for market. Meeting point walk-in at Homestead Cafe UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for 8-12.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Market wandering suits 8-12; meet at Homestead Cafe courtyard before circulating.
venues.capacity_notes: Family-run weekly food and farmers market launched March 2022 with local trader and produce focus.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the strongest Bromley market because it combines maker/producer browsing with an approved PAINTS park anchor. It has enough food variety for low-pressure conversation. The mansion, lake and courtyard add visual texture without needing a formal booking.
venues.anecdote_story: The park is a historic designed landscape; the market launched in March 2022 as a family-run weekly food and farmers market with local trader focus.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Produce, hot food, local traders, pastries, coffee and park/courtyard setting.
venues.safety_notes: Beckenham Place Park is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist; daytime market only. Keep meeting point at Homestead Cafe and avoid dusk dispersal through woodland.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by transit is plausible but must be verified for event day; no closer same-category market found.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and park-event disruption; backup is transit rather than walking.

venues.opening_hours: Official market site: Sundays 10:00-16:00. Crystal Palace Park Trust: Sundays 10:00-15:00, 16:00 in summer, may close in severe weather. Host must confirm date-specific close time.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; N/A for market. Meeting point Brown & Green Life is a cafe and walk-in/group tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 as a circulating market day; meet by Brown & Green Life/Penge Gate.
venues.capacity_notes: Weekly food market in Crystal Palace Park with seasonal produce, organic butchers, fish, bread, cakes, cheese, natural wines, craft beer and award-winning street food.
venues.why_fits_paints: The market has the rare combination of food, natural wine/craft beer, and a spectacular approved park setting. It is visually memorable because the dinosaurs and lakes are minutes away. It gets a small obviousness penalty because Crystal Palace is one of south London's better-known weekend circuits.
venues.anecdote_story: Crystal Palace Park contains the world's first dinosaur sculptures, created by Benjamin Waterhouse Hawkins in the 1850s; the park market gives that Victorian oddity a contemporary food anchor.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Seasonal fruit and veg, organic meat, fish, bread, cakes, cheese, charcuterie, olives, pasta, kimchi, honey, natural wines, craft beer, gin and street food.
venues.safety_notes: Crystal Palace Park is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist; daytime market only. Keep start/end at Penge Gate/Brown & Green Life and avoid after-dark park crossings.
venues.backup_venue: Crystal Palace Food Market, Haynes Lane
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 12-15 min walk/transit depending route - host to verify on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather closure possible; check social feeds before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: My Farmers Market and Visit Chislehurst: 3rd Sunday monthly, 10:00-14:00. Next-date page listed 2026 dates through June; future dates after July require confirmation.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for 8-12 if host treats it as a short browse plus coffee/pub debrief.
venues.capacity_notes: Over 20 stalls regularly attending per market operator; produce grown, raised or made by traders per Visit Chislehurst.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has genuine local-producer credentials and a Chislehurst village feel. The event is more practical than visually striking, so it sits below the park markets. The lack of a same-category backup inside 15 minutes makes it operationally fragile.
venues.anecdote_story: Set up by the Chislehurst Business Group; moved to Hornbrook House Car Park in 2012 to gain more space.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local produce, fish, bread, cheese, sauces, tea, cider, bakery and seasonal stalls.
venues.safety_notes: High Street daytime market; normal village-centre safety. Avoid using Chislehurst Common as an event meeting point because it is not on the PAINTS Creative Walk safety list.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying same-category market/craft destination found within 15 min walk or transit.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying backup within PAINTS market radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Monthly cadence and no tight backup; verify future 2026 dates directly.

venues.opening_hours: Park official page: open 24 hours, 365 days. Homestead Cafe: open daily from 08:00, closing varies and Google is stated as current; host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park walk; N/A for park. Homestead Cafe meeting/weather backup walk-in for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for 8-12 daytime walking with cafe start and optional courtyard debrief.
venues.capacity_notes: Use a 2-3 hour route: Homestead courtyard/stables, Mansion exterior and artist studios context, lake/wetlands, ancient woodland/meadow edge.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Bromley's cleanest Creative Walk because the park is explicitly PAINTS-approved and has varied stops within a manageable loop. It balances landscape, architecture, and social pauses. The Homestead Cafe gives a weather fallback within the start area.
venues.anecdote_story: Beckenham Place Park was opened as a public park in 1929 after London County Council purchased it from the Cator Estate; the Mansion now houses studios, classes, markets and a cafe/bar.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Stops: Homestead courtyard and restored Georgian stable cafe; Mansion exterior and creative studios context; lake/wetlands; woodland/meadow edge.
venues.safety_notes: Park is on PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist - confirmed. Daytime only; avoid woodland/darker internal paths near dusk and end at the cafe/courtyard.
venues.backup_venue: The Homestead Cafe / Mansion Cafe-Bar area
venues.risks_watchouts: Cafe closing varies by day/events; confirm before publishing. Park itself is 24-hour but PAINTS use should be daytime.

venues.venue_name: Crystal Palace Park Dinosaurs Creative Walk
venues.opening_hours: Dinosaur area is free during park opening hours; Brown & Green Life official cafe hours 08:30-16:30 weekdays, 08:30-17:00 weekends, kitchen closes 16:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park walk; N/A for park. Brown & Green Life group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for a daytime 8-12 person visual walk, with cafe start and clear anchor stops.
venues.capacity_notes: Use a 2-3 hour route: Brown & Green/Penge Gate, Lower Lake, Dinosaur Court, Italian Terraces/regeneration works, new dinosaur playground/visitor-centre context.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday 10:00-12:30; avoid dusk and winter late afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: Few London walks offer a better story object than the world's first dinosaur sculptures. The route is visual, odd, safe-listed and easy to talk through. It takes a small obviousness penalty because Crystal Palace Park is a well-known south-London landmark.
venues.anecdote_story: The Crystal Palace Dinosaurs were made in the 1850s by Benjamin Waterhouse Hawkins with scientific direction from Richard Owen; Historic England notes the Grade I listed sculptures and restoration efforts.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Stops: Brown & Green Life cafe; Lower Lake; Grade I dinosaur sculptures/geological court; terraces/regeneration context; dinosaur playground opened in March 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Park is on PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist - confirmed. Daytime only; avoid crossing the park after dark, with external-road route back to Penge West/Crystal Palace.
venues.backup_venue: Brown & Green Life, Crystal Palace Park
venues.risks_watchouts: Park Trust notes severe weather can affect park market; for walk, host should check restoration closures around dinosaur/terrace works.

venues.opening_hours: Official site: open Wed-Sat 09:30-17:00, last entry 16:30; exhibition Manasa Chitra runs 26 Jun-14 Nov 2026. Public holiday/install changes UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum entry appears free/public during opening hours; timed-entry not found. Group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 if host keeps tone careful and reflective; call ahead because the museum is on a working hospital site.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum and Bethlem Gallery share an Art Deco building on Bethlem Royal Hospital grounds.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the borough's standout museum/gallery day: the current exhibition is recent, visual, emotionally rich and press-validated. It offers art, archive, mental-health history and cross-cultural storytelling without relying on spectacle. The same-building gallery gives the rare qualifying backup.
venues.anecdote_story: The current exhibition links 1950s occupational-therapy drawings from Bangalore Mental Hospital with contemporary Bengaluru artists with lived experience of mental distress; the older works travelled through the friendship of M.V. Govindaswamy and psychiatrist Wilhelm Mayer-Gross.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Manasa Chitra: the Art of Mental Health from Bengaluru to Bethlem, plus museum collection works including art connected to mental-health histories.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime museum visit on hospital grounds; brief attendees clearly on respectful conduct and arrival route. Avoid evening use due to setting and transit distance.
venues.risks_watchouts: Working hospital context requires sensitive hosting; group access and any install closures must be confirmed.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Excellent independent bookshop with events and strong literary identity, but no on-site cafe/coffee partner literally next door was verified, so it fails the raised Books & Coffee rule.

Strong local heritage museum, but the PAINTS museum/gallery rule requires paintings, sculpture, textiles, ceramics or craft plus a notable current exhibition with recent press; current programming found was talks/events rather than a qualifying art/craft exhibition.
Historically interesting, but Bromley Council says it is closed to the public until refurbishment works complete, so it fails opening/operational gates for current sourcing.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Crystal Palace Food Market, Haynes Lane
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Good market and useful backup to Crystal Palace Park Market, but its listed address is Upper Norwood SE19 under Croydon Council rather than clearly in Bromley district; retained as backup only.
Promising community/craft market identity, but the live page found was for a 2025 date rather than current 2026 operating cadence; failed current operational verification.
Rejected because it is a Wetherspoon pub; PAINTS v6 excludes chains and templated venues for Tier A and this does not clear the PAINTS aesthetic bar.
Reopened under Craft Union/Stonegate sports-pub positioning with Sky/TNT racing screens; too sports-led and chain-adjacent for PAINTS Show & Tell Drinks.
Local mention suggests a book exchange, but no official/current source verified a substantive books wall, resident bookseller, or literary programme; fails Books & Coffee raised bar.
Not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list, so it cannot be used as a Creative Walk route or park meeting point even if locally attractive.
